commit_message_summary: feat(split): mark file SPLITTING in catalog, gate writers, verify MVCC read isolation (2b.1.3)
test_spec_result: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestSplittingStatusIsolation: PASS (5x repeats + included in full package -v run)
verifier_focus_pointers: engine/split/orchestrate.go: BeginSplit/EndSplit/AbortSplit/AdmitWrite and the private transitionStatus helper
engine/split/orchestrate_test.go: TestSplittingStatusIsolation, esp. reader_snapshot_unaffected_by_splitting and concurrent_writers_and_split_race subtests
architecture-discovery.md's 'Crash/stuck-SPLITTING gap' section -- confirm this is correctly scoped out (not silently missed)

architecture-discovery.md's scope-boundary section vs issue #12 -- confirm EndSplit(fileID, StatusSplit) is the correct minimal hook for #12 to call, without this subtask overreaching into #12's territory
known_non_blocking_notes: AdmitWrite is not yet wired into engine/catalog/content.go's ContentStore.Append (by design, out of scope per issue #10's impacted-modules list for 2b.1.3) -- flagged for a future subtask.

No automatic recovery for a SPLITTING status left behind by a crashed split holder (FileGuard state is process-lifetime-scoped, same class of gap as catalog.Catalog's own documented empty-index-on-restart limitation) -- recommend tracking in .cdr/memory/pending.md, likely to be addressed alongside issue #12's crash-injection test (TestSplitAtomicCommit).
